login.title: Welcome back
login.subtitle: Sign in to continue your HR system design
login.promo_title: Design your HR system with consulting-grade precision.
login.promo_subtitle: Transform your HR strategy with our step-by-step guided approach. Build organization structures, performance systems, and compensation frameworks.

register.title: Create HR Manager Account
register.subtitle: Register to start designing your HR system
register.password_hint: Use at least 10 characters. Uppercase letters are not required.
register.promo_description: Transform your HR strategy with our step-by-step guided approach. Build organization structures, performance systems, and compensation frameworks with consulting-grade precision.

diagnosis_review.completeSurveyFirst: Complete the CEO survey first, then continue.
diagnosis_review.completeRequiredBeforeVerification: Complete all required diagnosis sections before verification.

hero.title: Design Your Precise HR System
hero.description: We revolutionize how SMEs build HR frameworks. Our step-by-step guided approach recreates professional consulting work within a modern SaaS platform.

everything.title: Everything You Need to Build a Complete HR System
everything.description: Our platform guides you through each step with consulting-grade logic and rule-based recommendations.

organization.title: Organization Design
organization.description: Structure your company with functional, team-based, divisional, or matrix organizations.

job_analysis.title: Job Analysis
job_analysis.description: Analyze and define roles, responsibilities, and performance factors for each position.

performance.title: Performance Management System
performance.description: Design KPI, MBO, OKR, or BSC-based performance evaluation frameworks.

compensation.title: Compensation System
compensation.description: Build competitive pay structures through merit, incentives, and role-based differentiation.

why.title: Why Choose HR Path-Finder?
why.description: We replicate the structured approach of professional HR consulting, making it accessible to companies without dedicated HR planning teams.
items: Complete audit trail for all decisions

role_based.title: Role-Based Access
role_based.description: CEO, HR Manager, and Consultant each have specific permissions and views.

visual.title: Visual Dashboard
visual.description: View your entire HR system at a glance with professional visualizations.

validation.title: Logical Validation
validation.description: The system blocks incompatible choices to ensure consistent HR design.

collaboration.title: Collaborative Workflow
collaboration.description: CEO and HR Manager work together with clear handoffs and approvals.

cta.title: Ready to Design Your HR System?
cta.description: Start your free trial today and experience scalable consulting-grade HR design from prototype to full implementation.

hero_revision.badge: The engine that drives organizational growth through HR policy design
hero_revision.sub: Build internal HR capability and get full expert policy design at about one-quarter the cost of traditional consulting.
hero_revision.firm_sub: SaaS by a professional consulting firm
hero_revision.secondary_button: SaaS made by a professional consulting firm, Better Company →

trust_bar.item1: Policy design platform by Better Company, a professional HR consulting firm
trust_bar.item2: Policy design completed within about one month on average
trust_bar.item3: Strong cost advantage over traditional consulting

process.description: Each step feeds the next. When all four steps are complete, an HR consultant certifies your system with a tailored HR system report.

lead.highlight: We precisely sort and structure your company’s HR data

step_01.title: Organization diagnosis
step_01.desc: Diagnose your current organizational structure, job distribution, and headcount composition.

step_02.title: Business philosophy
step_02.desc: Clarify your organization’s core values and strategic direction.

step_03.title: Job analysis
step_03.desc: Systematically align your internal job families and roles.

step_04.title: Performance system design
step_04.desc: Design evaluation processes and goal management based on job characteristics.

step_05.title: Compensation structure design
step_05.desc: Build a competitive compensation structure based on compensation benchmarks.

trust_cards.description:
Pathfinder is not just a form tool. Your inputs become the basis for consultant design, and the result comes back as a reviewed HR system report.

"The system designs; the consultant certifies the report."
trust_cards.card1_desc: Enter organization structure, job definitions, performance framework, and compensation criteria step by step. Faster than handing materials to an external consultant.
trust_cards.card2_desc: A senior HR consultant designs the system to fit your industry, size, and culture using the data you provided.
trust_cards.card3_title: Delivered as an HR system report
trust_cards.card3_desc: A consultant-signed HR system report is delivered as a PDF, ready for immediate use in-house.

benchmark_section.description:
Pathfinder compares your grade- and role-based pay to industry, target competitors, and similar segments. With your company at 100, you see where the market sits.

This analysis is included in the HR system report.
benchmark_section.insight: Your compensation level is about 78% of the overall industry and 85% of target competitors.

cta_block.title: Design your company’s HR system today.
cta_block.description: If your company has 20–300 employees, Pathfinder is the fastest and most expert route to HR system design.

benchmark.title: Compensation Level Relative Index
benchmark.summary: Your company's compensation level is 78% compared to the overall industry and 84% compared to target competitors.

benchmark_card.subtitle: Annual Total Pay Level (Your company = 100) — market comparison

insight.strong: About 22% less competitive vs. the industry
insight.suffix: This analysis is included in your HR system report and delivered to you.

footer.left: As of Q2 2025 · Company size: 74 employees

roi.title: Pathfinder vs. Traditional Consulting
roi.description: The quality of deliverables is the same. What changes is cost, time, and internal capability.

table.note: * Timeline and cost may vary depending on scope and team involvement.

row_2.label: Cost
row_2.bad: Tens of millions KRW ~ hundreds of millions KRW

trust_section.description: The system designs, and consultants validate it in the final report.

hr_dashboard.start_building: Start building your company's HR system.

hr_dashboard.waiting_for_ceo_survey: Waiting for CEO to complete the survey...

steps.diagnosis_desc: Input company information, business profile, workforce details, and organizational culture.
steps.job_analysis_desc: Define job roles, responsibilities, competencies, and organizational mapping.
steps.performance_desc: Design evaluation units, performance management methods, and assessment structures.
steps.compensation_desc: Define compensation structure, differentiation methods, and incentive components.
steps.final_dashboard_desc: Design Progress summary — review all stages and submit for CEO approval.

completion.title: Completion Notification
completion.line1_prefix: The initial HR system design for
completion.line2: Once the consultant review and final report upload are finished, a separate email notification will be sent to the HR Manager of {{company}}.
completion.line3: Please use the link provided in the email to review the report and decide whether to request revisions or proceed with final confirmation.

comment.title: Consultant Brief Comment
comment.empty: No comment has been added yet.

missing_report.title: Report Not Uploaded Yet
missing_report.description: A consultant is reviewing your company's entire design draft and writing a report. An alarm email will be sent to the HR manager upon completion, so please refer to this.

contact.title: Contact Consultant
contact.description: Please contact your consultant or admin team for revision requests before final confirmation.

final_confirm.title: Proceed with Final Confirmation?
final_confirm.description: Once you proceed with final confirmation, the current HR system will be officially established, and further modifications or consultant review will no longer be available.

ceo_review_diagnosis.warning_not_submitted: Diagnosis must be submitted by HR before you can save changes or confirm. Ask your HR Manager to complete and submit the Diagnosis step, then return here to review and save.

success.title: Thank you
success.message:
Thank you for your time. Your part of the process is now complete.

1. Next Step: Our HR Manager will now begin drafting the system design based on your responses.
2. Final Report: Once the draft is finalized, a professional consultant's report will be delivered within 2 weeks.

We appreciate your patience while we prepare the best results for you.

review.hero_subtitle: Review submitted KPIs across all teams. Approve or request revisions per organization.
review.toolbar_hint: Review each team KPI · add comments when requesting revision · finalize when all are approved
review.no_kpis: No KPIs submitted yet. Leaders must complete their review first.
review.revision_comment_placeholder: Enter revision comments for this organization...
review.alert_warn: Average team weight is {{value}}%. Set each organization to 100% before finalizing.
review.alert_warn_per_org: Each organization must have KPI weights totaling exactly 100%. One or more teams are above or below 100% — adjust before finalizing.
review.alert_idle: Review each team KPI, then approve or request revision.
review.admin_usage_body: Review submitted company KPIs, request revision per organization, or finalize approved KPIs. For default KPI catalogs before project data exists, use KPI Templates.
review.revision_alert: Please add at least one revision comment.

legacy.revision_sent: Revision request sent to organization leader.
legacy.not_all_approved_confirm: Not all organizations have been approved. Do you want to finalize anyway?
legacy.review_desc: Review and finalize the KPIs submitted by each organization from a company-wide perspective.
legacy.guide_1_body: Are they aligned with the company's overall strategy? Ensure each organizational KPI is clearly linked to key strategic priorities.
legacy.guide_2_body: Is there an appropriate balance? Review whether responsibilities are fairly distributed.
legacy.guide_3_body: Are there any overlaps or gaps in accountability? Ensure no redundancy and no important areas left without ownership.
legacy.guide_4_body: Is the scope practical and manageable? Confirm that the number of KPIs is reasonable.
legacy.dialog_desc: Review and manage KPIs for this organization. You can approve, request revisions, or send feedback to the organization leader.
legacy.view_only: This is a view-only mode. To make changes, please access this section from the CEO dashboard.

outcome_influence.title: Outcome Influence
outcome_influence.question: Do these KPIs directly influence the team's core outcomes?
outcome_influence.hint: Verify they are tied to real results (revenue, quality, speed) — not just activity metrics like meeting attendance.

job_relevance.title: Job Relevance
job_relevance.question: Do these KPIs align with the responsibilities of this team?
job_relevance.hint: Check that no item overlaps with another team's scope or falls outside what this team can realistically control.

measurability.title: Measurability
measurability.question: Can all KPIs be measured with objective criteria or numbers?
measurability.hint: Ensure each KPI has a clearly defined formula, a designated measurement owner, and a set frequency.

data_availability.title: Data Availability
data_availability.question: Is the data needed for measurement actually accessible?
data_availability.hint: Confirm that data sources (systems, reports, etc.) exist and can be accessed on a regular basis.

hero.title: Job Analysis Overview
hero.desc: Define job standards and role expectations for your organization. Complete each step in sequence to build a consultant-ready job framework.
hero.before_body: This stage is not intended to redesign your current organizational structure. It organizes current job standards and role expectations to support later performance and compensation design. All answers are baseline inputs and are kept confidential.

step2.title: Job List Selection

context.title: Diagnosis Context
context.subtitle: Based on your Step 1-2 responses
context.why_body: Jobs marked Core are based on your profile. Pre-selected roles reflect standard essentials.

step2.purpose_text: Define the scope of jobs that exist in your company based on industry characteristics. This step confirms and finalizes which jobs will be designed and structured.
step2.info_text: Based on your industry and organizational size, we suggest common roles in similar companies. Select only jobs currently operating in your company and add unique roles if needed. Pre-configured list for {{industry}} company ({{size}}), {{framework}}.
step2.sidebar_framework_with: with a formal job classification framework
step2.sidebar_framework_without: without a formal job classification framework
step2.complete_min: Select at least {{min}} jobs to continue

step3.title: Job Definition
step3.intro: For each selected role, a Job Definition Document is created and finalized, including: Job Description, Job Specification, Job Competency Levels, and Critical Success Factors (CSFs). The job standards defined at this stage serve as the foundation for the subsequent design of the performance management system and the compensation system.
step3.no_jobs: No jobs selected. Please go back to Job List Selection.
step3.category_guide: Category guide: Strategic – goals & direction. Process – how work gets done. Operational – day-to-day execution. The category determines placement in the Finalization matrix.
step3.csf_description_placeholder: Describe this CSF and expected outcome
step3.csfs_empty: No CSFs yet. Click "+ Add CSF" to add one.

step4.title: Finalization of Job Definitions
step4.intro: The job structure and Job Definition documents, reviewed and refined during the Job Definition stage, are finalized below. The finalized job standards are used as baseline inputs for the subsequent design of the performance management system and the compensation system.
step4.alert_strong: all jobs will be finalized globally.
step4.alert_after: This is a global confirmation of the entire job configuration — not per-job. Review each job card carefully before proceeding.
step4.no_jobs: No job definitions found. Please go back to Job Definition to complete at least one role.
step4.no_csf: No critical success factors defined for this job.
step4.footer_roles: Job Definitions: {{count}} roles ready for finalization
step4.success_message: Progress is saved. Continue to Org Chart Mapping when you are ready.

step5.title: Organization Chart Mapping
step5.intro: The finalized Job Definition documents are mapped to your organizational structure, and the responsible owners for each organization unit and role are identified. This stage is not intended to change the organizational structure or make HR or personnel decisions. Drag jobs from the right panel and drop them into the appropriate unit.
step5.validation_hint: Type the unit name in the box next to the folder icon at the top of each card (e.g. "Division A", "Finance Team"), then click Continue again.
step5.empty_units: No organizational units yet. Click "+ Add Unit" to create one.
step5.unit_name_placeholder: Unit name (e.g. Division A, Finance Team)
step5.no_finalized_jobs: No finalized jobs. Complete Job Definition first.
step5.validation_unit_names: Please enter a name for every organizational unit in the unit name field (top row of each card, next to the folder icon).

step6.title: Step 6 — Review & Submit
step6.intro: Review all collected data before final submission. Once submitted, the Job Analysis step will be completed.
step6.matrix_subtitle: {{name}} · Stage 3 draft data (editable preview for confirmation)
step6.no_jobs_warning: No jobs have been finalized. Please go back to Finalization to complete at least one job.
step6.footer_review: Jobs: {{jobs}} · Org units: {{orgs}} · Review above, then submit.
step6.submit_err_jobs: Add at least one job definition before submitting.
step6.toast_desc: Your job analysis has been submitted.
step6.dialog_desc: All data has been submitted successfully.

expert_review.label: Next Step - Expert Review
expert_review.title: After submission, an HR consultant will review your design
expert_review.desc: Based on the draft you've designed, HR Path-Finder's expert consultants will review whether it fits your organization's reality and propose improvements where needed. The final performance management system is confirmed through a collaborative review process.

mbo.title: Management by Objectives (MBO)
mbo.desc: Sets clear quantitative targets and evaluates by achievement rate.
mbo.fit: Best for roles with clear numeric KPIs - Sales, Operations

okr.title: Objectives & Key Results (OKR)
okr.desc: Pairs ambitious Objectives with measurable Key Results.
okr.fit: Best for creative/strategic roles - R&D, Strategy, Engineering

bsc.title: Balanced Scorecard (BSC)
bsc.fit: Best for support roles - HR, Finance, Planning

performance_system_index.title: Performance System

performance_system_index.success_desc: Your performance design has been submitted for consultant review.

step_desc.snapshot: Answer 10 questions about your company's performance management approach

compensation_system.title: Compensation System
compensation_system.overview_desc: Define compensation structure, differentiation methods, and incentive components. Complete each step in sequence to build a consultant-ready compensation and benefits framework.
compensation_system.before_begin_desc: This stage builds on your job analysis and performance system. Your compensation snapshot answers will be used to tailor the base salary framework and subsequent steps. There are no right or wrong answers—your responses are baseline inputs for the compensation design. All inputs are confidential.
compensation_system.consultant_recommendation_desc: Your consultant has prepared a recommendation based on your performance system selection and company context.
compensation_system.submitted_desc: Step 4 is complete. Your consultant will review and the CEO can approve.
compensation_system.submitted_desc_ko: 4단계가 완료되었습니다. 컨설턴트가 검토하고 CEO가 승인할 수 있습니다.

pay_band_creator.err_target: Target must be greater than Min.
pay_band_creator.err_max: Max must be greater than Target.
pay_band_creator.err_senior: Senior grade max must be greater than entry grade max.
pay_band_creator.title: Pay Band Creator
pay_band_creator.subtitle: Enter grade steps and anchor values to generate the full pay band structure automatically.
pay_band_creator.scale_note: Adjusting values rescales all bands automatically.
pay_band_creator.chart_placeholder: Enter valid anchors to generate the band chart.
pay_band_creator.chart_hint: Set MIN / TARGET / MAX and senior MAX correctly to render the live chart.
pay_band_creator.overlap_warn: Some overlaps are below 15%. Please review fit for your structure.
pay_band_creator.continue_footer_hint: Use Continue at the bottom of the page to save and go to the next step.

base_salary_ui.title: Salary Structure
base_salary_ui.subtitle: Define the structural principles governing how base salaries are organized, adjusted, and differentiated across your organization.

tips.structure_type: The overarching framework for how base salaries are structured. Accumulated systems add increments; Non-accumulated reset to base each year; Hybrid combines both.
tips.adjustment_unit: How salary changes are expressed: as a percentage (%) or as an absolute amount in Korean Won (KRW).
tips.determination_standard: Pay Band: salaries set within defined min–max ranges per grade. Salary Table: a fixed matrix of salary amounts by grade and step.
tips.grouping: Single group: entire org reviewed simultaneously. Dual group: split by hire cohort timing to spread workload and budget.
tips.timing: The calendar month in which salary revisions take effect. Typically aligns with the performance review cycle completion.
tips.common_increase: Required: all employees receive a baseline increase (e.g., inflation-linked) in addition to performance adjustments. Not Required: increases are purely performance-driven.
tips.perf_diff: Strong: performance ratings drive large pay gaps. Moderate: meaningful but not extreme differences. No differentiation: uniform increases regardless of performance.

base_salary_ui.policy_p2: system, administering salary adjustments expressed in
base_salary_ui.policy_p3: . Individual salaries are determined against a
base_salary_ui.policy_p6: annually. A common baseline increase is
base_salary_ui.preview_empty: Select a Salary Structure Type to see a visual preview
base_salary_ui.preview_selected: Illustration only. Pay bands or the salary table are configured in the next step.
base_salary_ui.purpose_b1: Define how salary bands and pay grades are structured across the organization.
base_salary_ui.purpose_b2: Configure the adjustment unit, cycle, and performance linkage level.
base_salary_ui.purpose_b3: This configuration will auto-generate pay band tables and serve as the foundation for all compensation decisions.

step_desc.snapshot: Answer questions about your company's compensation philosophy and current approach.
step_desc.base_salary_framework: Define salary structure type, adjustment unit, cycle, and performance linkage.

step_desc.bonus_pool: Set bonus pool determination, eligibility, and allocation rules.
step_desc.benefits: Define benefits programs, strategic direction, and expense ratios.
step_desc.review: Final review and submission of your compensation system.

Complete this mapping to establish your organizational hierarchy before proceeding to the Performance Management stage.
